title: Model Bias in Medical Imaging
description: What are the leading causes and mitigation strategies for bias in AI models used for medical imaging diagnoses?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly articles that analyze how dataset composition, preprocessing pipelines, and model architecture choices contribute to systematic bias in AI‑driven medical imaging. Relevance is judged by coverage of bias detection techniques, quantitative evaluations across diverse populations, and evidence‑based mitigation strategies that align with regulatory guidelines and promote reproducibility.

title: Legacy Python Refactoring
description: How can I identify and resolve code smells in an older Python codebase to improve maintainability without breaking existing functionality?
narrative: The user seeks practical guidance on detecting common code smells—such as duplicated logic, long methods, and excessive globals—in a legacy Python project. They also want recommendations for refactoring techniques that preserve behavior, such as extracting methods, applying design patterns, and leveraging automated tooling. Relevance is judged by presence of actionable steps, example code snippets, and an emphasis on maintaining backward compatibility. The documents provided are cooking recipes and thus contain no related information.

title: Urban Electric Commuter
description: Which electric vehicle is best suited for a weekday commuter in a congested city, considering cost, range, and charging infrastructure?
narrative: The user is a city‑based professional who drives about 60 km per day, needs a vehicle that (1) costs under €35,000, (2) offers a highway‑rated range of at least 300 km, (3) supports 150 kW fast charging or higher to recharge during lunch breaks, (4) has a reputable battery warranty for at least 8 years, (5) includes essential safety features but not advanced autonomous driving, (6) provides good low‑speed maneuverability and parking aid, and (7) is backed by a network of service centers in major metropolitan areas. Relevance is judged by how well a document addresses or compares models that meet these criteria and provides up‑to‑date pricing, availability, or owner‑experienced data for city use.}

title: Smart Home Energy
description: How can I optimize energy usage in my smart home using modern devices and technologies?
narrative: The user is looking for actionable, evidence‑based strategies to reduce electricity consumption in a residential smart‑home setting. Relevant documents should include technical analyses, comparative studies of smart thermostats, appliances, and monitoring systems, as well as real‑world case studies that demonstrate measurable savings and explain implementation details.

title: VR PTSD Therapy
description: What is the evidence supporting the use of virtual reality therapy for treating post-traumatic stress disorder in military veterans?
narrative: The user is a researcher conducting a systematic review of interventions for PTSD among veterans. Relevance criteria include peer‑reviewed randomized controlled trials, quasi‑experimental studies, or meta‑analyses published within the last decade that specifically incorporate virtual reality (VR) therapy and compare it to standard care or other psychotherapeutic modalities. Studies must involve veteran populations and report clinically significant outcomes such as PTSD symptom severity, functional improvement, or dropout rates. Non‑VR interventions, studies on non‑military populations, or articles that discuss VR outside a therapeutic context are considered irrelevant.

title: Compact Eco‑Design Wood Stoves
description: Where can I find a small, efficient wood stove suitable for use on a boat or in a tiny house that fulfills RT2022 eco‑design standards?
narrative: The user is looking for information on compact wood stoves that are suitable for limited volumes (≈10–25 m²) such as a boat cabin, trailer, or tiny house. Relevance criteria include stove capacity, dimensions, fuel compatibility (solid wood, charcoal), compliance with eco‑design (RT2022 or similar) and practical installation details. Documents should focus on technical specifications and environmental certifications rather than general décor or unrelated product categories.

title: Avoiding Hidden Car Fees
description: What common hidden fees arise when buying a used car and how can I avoid or minimize them?
narrative: The user wants detailed information on the subtle additional costs that can surface during a used‑car purchase—such as dealer add‑ons, title transfer fees, inspection costs, and tax variations—and practical guidance on verifying, negotiating, and documenting these fees to protect their budget.

title: LEED Platinum Criteria
description: What are the essential requirements for a commercial office building to attain LEED Platinum certification?
narrative: The user is a sustainability consultant preparing a proposal for a mid-size office building and needs a detailed explanation of the LEED categories, point thresholds, and examples of successful projects. Relevance is based on documents that outline the LEED rating system, specific criteria for each credit, case studies, and guidance materials from the U.S. Green Building Council.

title: Right of Option Evaluation
description: Should I exercise my right of option to switch to higher unemployment allowance after returning to work?
narrative: The user is a recent ex‑employee who resumed work for six months and is now looking at the choice between keeping the remaining original ARE entitlements or opting for a higher but shorter benefit period under the reloaded rights. Relevance criteria include documents that discuss conditions for the right of option, calculations of new rates, eligibility thresholds (e.g., minimum 910 hours), procedural steps, and potential impact on overall benefit duration.

title: Biodegradable Microbeads
description: What are the most promising biodegradable alternatives to microbeads used in personal care products?
narrative: The user is researching sustainable replacements for microbeads in cosmetics and wants peer‑reviewed studies or credible industry reports that detail biodegradability, environmental impact, regulatory status, and real‑world usage. Relevance is determined by coverage of specific alternative materials, quantitative degradation data, certifications, or case studies; marketing brochures, generic overviews, or non‑scientific reports are considered irrelevant.

title: Sustainable Agriculture Practices
description: I want comprehensive information on the environmental and economic impacts of precision farming techniques compared to conventional methods in arid regions.
narrative: The user is a researcher interested in assessing whether precision agriculture reduces water usage and land degradation under arid climate conditions, seeking studies, case reports, and statistical analyses that quantify these impacts as well as economic feasibility for smallholders.

title: Urban Pollinator Gardens
description: What are effective strategies for creating pollinator-friendly gardens on small urban rooftops?
narrative: The user seeks detailed, evidence-based approaches to design and maintain a garden on a rooftop that supports bees and butterflies, including plant selection, soil amendments, water provision, and integration with building regulations. Relevance requires practical guidelines, case studies, and compliance information, not general gardening tips.

title: Eco‑city waste
description: What are the most effective circular waste‑management strategies implemented in European eco‑cities?
narrative: The user wants to evaluate and compare waste‑management approaches that reduce landfill use, increase recycling and upcycling, and foster the circular economy within European eco‑city contexts. Relevant documents should present evidence‑based studies, city reports, policy documents, or case studies that detail the strategies, metrics, outcomes, and lessons learned.

title: Zero‑Waste Packaging
description: What materials and designs can reduce the environmental impact of single‑use product packaging while maintaining cost and consumer convenience?
narrative: The user is a supply‑chain manager looking to replace traditional plastic bottles with fully recyclable or compostable options. Relevance is defined by technical specifications (material composition, barrier properties), lifecycle analysis data, certifications (BPI, FSC), real‑world case studies, and cost comparison studies published within the last five years. Documents that mention generic sustainability claims without measurable data or focus solely on marketing are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Productivity
description: What are the measurable effects of remote work on team productivity and employee satisfaction?
narrative: The user is looking for evidence‑based insights into how working remotely influences both output metrics (e.g., project completion times, quality indicators) and employee well‑being measures (e.g., engagement scores, turnover rates). Relevant documents should include empirical studies, industry reports, best‑practice guidelines, and case studies that assess productivity and satisfaction across various organizational contexts.

title: Foreign Artisan Startup
description: What steps must a non‑European artisan take to register and run a micro‑entrepreneurial craft business in France, including qualification, registration, language and financial support?
narrative: The user is a foreign craftsman interested in establishing a small‑scale manufacturing or repair shop in France. They need guidelines on the required qualifications, the registration process with the Trades Chamber, the need for a legal structure, translation of diplomas if not in French, possible tax or social security obligations, and available financial or training assistance. Relevance means documents that cover legal requirements for non‑EU artisans, micro‑entrepreneur application procedures, translation and recognition of qualifications, and support programs.

title: Arctic Navigation Methods
description: What navigation techniques were employed by Arctic indigenous peoples before modern GPS?
narrative: The user seeks detailed descriptions of pre‑modern navigation practices, including cultural context, instruments (e.g., star charts, oral lore, sled routes), and examples from various Arctic groups (e.g., Inuit, Nenets). Relevance requires inclusion of historical, ethnographic evidence and excludes modern technology or non‑indigenous practices.

title: Sustainable Potato Farming
description: How can I implement sustainable practices for potato cultivation that balance yield, soil health, and environmental impact?
narrative: The user is a farmer or agronomy student seeking evidence-based guidelines for integrated pest management, water‑efficient irrigation, and soil fertility preservation. Relevance criteria include peer‑reviewed research, extension manuals, and case studies from temperate zones that demonstrate measurable outcomes in yield and ecological indicators.

title: Urban Heritage Balance
description: What approaches can cities like Charenton‑le‑Pont use to protect historic landmarks while expanding modern infrastructure such as highways and transit corridors?
narrative: The user is a municipal planner researching how to integrate historic preservation with infrastructural development. They seek case studies, zoning strategies, community‑engagement methods, and measurable impacts on cultural, environmental, and economic factors. Relevant documents should provide policy examples, legislative frameworks, and best‑practice guidance for balancing heritage conservation with modern growth.

title: Home Energy Audit Timing
description: When and why should a homeowner schedule an energy audit, and how to evaluate its cost‑benefit?
narrative: The user wants to determine the optimal timing for a residential energy audit, understanding factors such as seasonal weather, home age, previous renovations, and the typical return on investment. Relevance criteria include detailed guidance on scheduling, cost estimates, projected savings calculations, case studies, and links to reputable audit providers. The information should help the user decide whether an audit is financially worthwhile for their specific situation.

title: Fashion Supply Transparency
description: What are the most effective methods to audit and verify sustainable practices in global apparel supply chains?
narrative: The user seeks detailed case studies, certification frameworks, data transparency tools, and policy recommendations for ensuring ethical sourcing, explicitly filtering out generic marketing claims.

title: Gaulish Historical Sites
description: What are the main archaeological sites of the Gauls in Burgundy and how can I plan a visit to them?
narrative: The user is planning a historical tour focused on the Gaulish heritage in Burgundy. They need detailed, up‑to‑date information on the key sites—Alésia, Autun (Augustodunum), Bibracte, and the Vix Treasure museum—such as exact locations, operating hours, admission fees, guided tour availability, and accessibility options. Relevance requires that documents provide concrete visiting details (opening times, ticket price, best travel routes, and any seasonal restrictions) rather than general historical summaries or unrelated tourist activities. The response should help in creating a practical itinerary that covers all major sites in a logical geographical sequence and includes practical travel advice for the user’s visit.

title: Workplace Urbanism
description: How has the rise of remote work affected urban planning decisions in major cities over the last decade?
narrative: The user seeks evidence on the interplay between the proliferation of remote working and changes in urban planning policies. Relevant documents should include academic studies, municipal planning reports, and policy briefs published from 2010 to the present that address changes in zoning, transportation, housing density, and public space usage in cities with populations exceeding 500,000. Non‑relevant material such as general tourism guides, car‑hire ads, or unrelated local business listings should be ignored.

title: Electric Micro‑Mobility Adoption
description: What are the main factors influencing the adoption of electric scooters and bikes in mid‑sized European cities?
narrative: The user is researching the uptake of electric micro‑mobility solutions in cities with populations between 100,000 and 500,000 residents. Relevance should be determined by information that identifies drivers (e.g., price, convenience, availability), barriers (e.g., regulation, safety concerns, infrastructure gaps), and policy recommendations that have been implemented or proposed in comparable urban contexts. Documents must describe real‑world initiatives, statistical data, or case studies and should not merely provide marketing material or unrelated marketing claims.

title: Urban Green Roofs
description: I want to know the latest research on how green roofs contribute to urban heat island mitigation, energy savings, and biodiversity.
narrative: The user seeks peer‑reviewed studies, data‑driven case studies, and technical specifications on designing and implementing green roofs in high‑rise buildings, with emphasis on materials, maintenance costs, and measured environmental benefits over time.

title: Climate Impact on Vineyards
description: How is climate change affecting grape yields and wine quality in French vineyards?
narrative: The user needs recent studies or reports that analyze how climatic factors such as temperature, precipitation, and extreme weather events influence grape composition, harvest dates, and overall vine health in French appellations. Relevance requires scientific rigor, data from 2015 onward, and discussion of adaptation strategies specific to French viticulture.

title: Urban Green Roofs
description: What are the economic and environmental benefits of implementing green roofs on high‑rise buildings?
narrative: The user is an urban planner evaluating whether to incorporate green roofs into new skyscraper construction. They need case studies, cost‑benefit analyses, energy‑savings data, stormwater‑management metrics, biodiversity impacts, and information on local regulations or incentives. Relevance is defined by documents that provide quantitative performance data, real‑world examples, policy context, and technology assessment relevant to high‑rise applications.

title: EV Tax Incentives
description: What federal tax incentives have been available to buyers of electric vehicles in the United States over the past decade, and how have they changed annually?
narrative: The user seeks a detailed, year‑by‑year account of U.S. federal tax credits for electric vehicles (EVs) from 2010 to 2023, focusing on the credit amount, eligibility requirements, phase‑out thresholds, and any legislative amendments. Relevance requires documents that provide a chronological or tabulated summary of these incentives, exclude state‑level incentives, hybrids, or unrelated automotive financial metrics.

title: Green Roof Policy
description: How do green roof regulations influence construction costs and environmental outcomes in major U.S. cities?
narrative: The user intends to write a comparative policy analysis on green roof legislation. Relevance is judged by the presence of empirical studies, city‑level cost breakdowns, and measurable environmental metrics (e.g., heat island mitigation, stormwater retention). The user seeks evidence that ties regulatory changes directly to economic impacts and quantifiable ecological benefits, excluding purely theoretical or advocacy‑centric reports.

title: Green Travel Funding
description: How can travelers secure financing for environmentally sustainable trips?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on financial products that explicitly support eco‑friendly travel—especially options such as green loans, carbon‑offset credit lines, and partnership programmes with sustainable tour operators. Relevance criteria include documents that describe eligibility, interest rates, repayment terms, guarantees of environmental impact, and any special incentives or collaborations that promote low‑carbon travel experiences.

title: Electric vs Gasoline Rental
description: What are the financial and operational differences between renting a used electric car and a gasoline car in a metropolitan area?
narrative: The user is a prospective car‑sharing owner who wants to decide whether to purchase a used electric vehicle or keep a gasoline model for private rental. They need information that covers expected monthly revenue, insurance costs, maintenance expenses, depreciation rates, charging infrastructure costs, and any city‑specific regulations affecting rentals. Relevance requires documents that provide detailed cost comparisons, revenue estimates, insurance policy data, and regulatory guidance, with emphasis on the nuances of electric‑vehicle operations versus gasoline‑vehicle operations.

title: Cannabis Alcohol
description: What are the testing methods, legal thresholds, and penalties for driving under the influence of both cannabis and alcohol in France?
narrative: The user wants to know how combined cannabis and alcohol use is evaluated at checkpoints, what the legal limits are, and what sanctions apply, focusing on French regulations and practical enforcement details.

title: Green Roofing Systems
description: Which green roof technologies provide optimal stormwater management and energy savings for mid-sized urban buildings in temperate climates?
narrative: The user is a facility manager seeking to implement green roofs and wants evidence on performance, cost, and case studies. Relevance requires inclusion of studies evaluating stormwater metrics, energy savings, lifecycle analysis, and real-world implementations in temperate urban settings.

title: Assessing Telemedicine Adoption in Rural Southeast Asia
description: How have rural communities in Southeast Asia adopted telemedicine services, and what factors influence their usage?
narrative: The user is a public health researcher seeking detailed case studies, adoption rates, implementation barriers and facilitators, and policy implications related to telemedicine in rural Southeast Asia. Relevance is based on peer‑reviewed academic articles, government and NGO reports, and longitudinal studies that provide quantitative and qualitative evidence, while purely news items or anecdotal blog posts are considered less relevant.

title: Urban Greenspace Biodiversity
description: I want to understand what human activities most significantly influence biodiversity in urban greenspaces in North American cities.
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive, evidence‑based information on the specific anthropogenic factors—such as recreational use intensity, landscaping practices, pollution levels, and urban heat island effects—that shape species composition, diversity indices, and ecological resilience within city parks, community gardens, and other green infrastructure. Relevance should be judged by reports that quantify activity‑biodiversity relationships using spatial analysis, longitudinal studies, or case studies, and that provide actionable guidelines for planners and conservationists seeking to mitigate negative impacts and enhance habitat quality.

title: Remote Work Desktop
description: What hardware and software configuration should I use for a desktop that supports virtualization, video conferencing, and high security needs for remote work?
narrative: The user is a professional who works remotely and requires a desktop capable of running multiple virtual machines, handling high‑definition video conferencing, and enforcing strict security protocols such as VPNs and hardware encryption. Relevance criteria include detailed information on processor performance (e.g., core count, hyper‑threading), memory size and ECC support, NVMe SSD/SATA speed, GPU passthrough for VR/graphics tasks, networking features (wired/Wi‑Fi, QoS), and OS‑level security settings (BitLocker, TPM, secure boot).

title: Black‑white film comparison
description: Which historical black‑white photographic films best reproduce grain and contrast for studio portraits?
narrative: The user is a portrait photographer looking to select a silver film that achieves a specific aesthetic. Relevance requires documents that analyze and compare the grain structure, contrast gradation, ISO performance, and typical use cases of classic films such as Kodak Tri‑X, Ilford FP4 Plus, Fujifilm Neopan, and similar, along with any modern digital conversion recommendations or preservation considerations.

title: Pet-friendly beach resorts Southeast Asia
description: Which affordable family‑friendly beach resorts in Southeast Asia accommodate pets and offer activities for children?
narrative: A family of four, traveling with a dog, is looking for resorts that balance cost, pet policies, and children’s entertainment. Relevant results must be in Southeast Asia, priced under $200 per night, with documented pet‑friendly amenities and child‑friendly facilities such as kids’ clubs, pool activities, or guided tours.

title: Remote Work Commuting
description: What are the long‑term effects of widespread remote work on urban commuting patterns, traffic congestion, and public transportation systems?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based analyses and case studies that explore how sustained remote work influences the frequency, distance, and mode of city commutes, and how these changes impact road congestion, transit ridership, and related infrastructure planning. Relevance criteria include peer‑reviewed studies, government reports, and reputable industry analyses that discuss measurable outcomes over multiple years or decades.

title: Blockchain Supply Transparency
description: How can blockchain technology be applied to enhance transparency and traceability in complex international supply chains for carbon certification?
narrative: The user is a supply‑chain analyst looking for concrete, actionable information on blockchain implementations that improve traceability of goods and their environmental impact. They need case studies, technical guidelines, and architectural diagrams illustrating how to record provenance, verify carbon‑offset claims, and integrate certifications into existing logistics workflows. Relevance is determined by articles that discuss real deployments, address regulatory compliance, and evaluate technical and cost challenges of integrating blockchain into multination supply chain operations.

title: Elderly Tech Preparedness
description: What are the best practices for helping elderly family members set up smartphone backups and recovery options?
narrative: The user wants guidance to ensure elderly relatives can recover their data and maintain security on their phones, including instructions for backups, password reset, and protection from phishing scams. Relevant answers should mention secure backup methods, user-friendly restoration guides, and anti-phishing measures tailored to seniors.

title: Alternative Lamp Fuels
description: Which alternative fuels can be safely used in traditional oil lamps for home lighting?
narrative: A home‑prepping enthusiast wants to replace commercial lamp oil with more readily available or environmentally friendly options. They need reliable information on fuel properties such as flash point, viscosity, odor, and combustion efficiency; compatibility with common wick materials; safety precautions for storage and handling; and evidence of long‑term stability. Relevance will be judged by documents that detail empirical tests, chemical analyses, and user‑reported performance of fuels like kerosene, would‑be vegetable oils, or biofuels in oil‑lamp applications.

title: Coastal Erosion Projections
description: What are the recent scientific projections for coastal erosion impacts along the Pacific shoreline over the next two decades?
narrative: The user is planning a long‑term coastal development project and seeks up‑to‑date, peer‑reviewed studies that quantify erosion rates, identify high‑risk segments, and model future sea‑level scenarios. Relevant documents should include recent climatology and geological reports, GIS mapping outputs, and consensus analyses from authoritative national and international bodies. Unrelated tourism or travel guides are not pertinent.

title: Savory Quince Recipes
description: Looking for quick, oil‑free recipes that use quince in a savory context.
narrative: The user is a health‑conscious home cook who wants to explore savory uses of quince, excluding sweet dishes and high‑fat preparations. They specifically want recipes that can be prepared under 30 minutes, use no added cooking oil, and pair well with flavors such as goat cheese, rosemary, or lamb. Relevance criteria include clear, step‑by‑step instructions, a list of necessary ingredients, and evidence that quince is a primary component in a savory dish.

title: Legacy Microservice Cleaning
description: What are the best practices for refactoring legacy code in a microservices architecture?
narrative: The user is a senior engineer looking for actionable strategies to gradually modernize legacy services while minimizing downtime. Relevant documents should cover incremental refactoring techniques, service decomposition patterns, data migration approaches, testing and monitoring strategies, and risk mitigation examples.
